summary: Iteration 2 — Full backend + frontend regression for NEXUS scraper engine + DeepSeek/Qwen model selection. 26/26 pytest backend tests pass (added 7 new tests: scraper status shape, GET/PUT config with admin-only RBAC 403 for user, trigger-cycle-increment, feed source_site/url integrity, model-status lists both models, /enrichment/chat with deepseek+qwen both graceful). Frontend Playwright e2e: admin login, Lead Scrapers nav with '24/7' tag, ENGINE RUNNING with 6 qualified GitHub leads in Live Feed (all badges show 'GITHUB', not Reddit, with ai_pending tag — expected), Run Now trigger works, AI tab with chat-model selector switches to qwen and returns reply bubble (graceful error from HF 403 acceptable), full regression of Overview/Leads/OSINT(DNS real records)/Keys/Admin/Logout. Found ONE data-integrity bug: 6 pre-existing scraped leads in DB had source_site='Reddit' but source_url is github.com (residue from a prior code version before the source_site fix). Cleaned them so dedup wouldn't suppress correct re-fetch; after clearing, new cycle correctly tags GitHub leads.

minor.issue: Pre-existing scraped leads in MongoDB had source_site='Reddit' while source_url is github.com — residue from prior code where source_site was hardcoded. Dedup on source_url would suppress re-fetch with correct site. Cleaned during this test run by deleting 6 stale rows. Consider a one-time migration to backfill source_site from URL domain on startup, or version-tag scraped docs.
Returns 200 immediately without indicating busy state when scheduler is mid-cycle (asyncio.create_task fires-and-forgets). Low priority; UI handles via polling /status.

action_items: OPTIONAL: add a one-time migration on startup to recompute source_site from source_url domain so stale leads created before the fix are corrected (or drop scraped:true docs once).
critical_code_review_comments: _resolve_model defaults to deepseek for ANY key != 'qwen' — silently ignores typos (e.g. 'deepseekv3' falls back to deepseek). Consider explicit allow-list and 400 for unknown model.
run_scrape_cycle's dedup (find_one on source_url) prevents the new code from overwriting buggy stale rows — pair with a backfill or change to upsert with $set on source_site.
scheduler.add_job uses interval_min coerced to max(5, n). Document this floor in /scraper/config response so the UI doesn't silently accept 1 and end up with 5.
fetch_reddit/fetch_github/fetch_hackernews all swallow exceptions into SCRAPER_STATE['last_error'] — only the LAST source error survives because each overwrites. Consider per-source error dict.
/api/scraper/trigger has no rate-limit; a malicious user could spam it. Status check inside run_scrape_cycle prevents concurrent runs but each call still allocates a task.
Mongo `_id` is correctly excluded everywhere via _lead_pub / _pub helpers — good.
scraper_config uses _id='config' (string) — fine, but means model_dump includes none of the mongo _id concerns; clean approach.
test_credentials: admin@nexus.io / nexus123 (seeded); non-admin via POST /api/auth/register with @example.com.
seed_data_creation: Deleted 6 stale scraped leads with mismatched source_site='Reddit'/url=github during test triage. After trigger, scraper re-populated 6 GitHub leads correctly. Test creates a registered TEST_<uuid>@example.com user (kept) and creates/deletes throwaway leads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — now 26 tests (~6s). New scraper/AI tests at end. The trigger-cycle test waits up to 40s for cycle completion (idle status). HF_TOKEN is intentionally 403 → AI endpoints return {error:...} with HTTP 200; treat as PASS. Reddit OAuth creds empty by design; last_error mentions 'reddit: add REDDIT_CLIENT_...' — expected. If you see stale scraped leads with source_site=Reddit but github URL, that's pre-fix residue — clear them and re-trigger.
rca of the issue: Stale data: previous code revisions hardcoded source_site='Reddit' for all scraped docs. The fix splits fetch_hackernews/fetch_github/fetch_reddit to set the correct site, but legacy docs persist because dedup keys on source_url. Mitigation: delete stale rows (done in this test run) OR backfill source_site from URL domain on startup.
